problem.summary: Compute a 256-bin histogram from uint32 input values. The baseline atomically increments global bins for every element. The optimized seed privatizes the 256 bins in shared memory per block, then merges block-private bin counts into global memory.
outputs: histogram[0:256] contains exact counts for input[i] & 255
constraints: counts are expected to fit in uint32_t for benchmarked shapes
input distribution strongly affects atomic contention and should be recorded with every result

verification.method: CPU reference histogram over the same generated input, exact per-bin comparison, and total-count check. Ported from the CUDA corpus; rerun on ROCm hardware before marking passed.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. CUDA-origin timing artifacts were removed; rerun on ROCm hardware before making performance claims.
